message:
perf(fcp-raptorq): coalesce post-K decode retries (br-yu0l5)

crates/fcp-raptorq/src/decode.rs add_symbol previously called
try_reconstruct on EVERY symbol once received_count >= K.
try_reconstruct rebuilds the full InactivationDecoder state, clones
every buffered symbol into a ReceivedSymbol row, and allocates K'-K
virtual padding rows on each attempt. On a lossy link needing K+R
symbols to reach full rank, the total work was
sum_{i=K}^{K+R} O(i * symbol_size) — a throughput cliff the moment
the first symbol is lost.

## Fix

Add a retry coalescer to `RaptorQDecoder`:

    last_attempted_at_count: u32
    post_k_retry_step:       u32   // starts at 1, doubles on
                                   // InsufficientSymbols, cap at
                                   // MAX_POST_K_RETRY_STEP (32)

A full decode attempt now runs only when `should_attempt_decode(esi)`
returns true, which is:

  (a) first K-crossover (last_attempted_at_count == 0)  — ALWAYS
  (b) repair-symbol arrival (esi >= K)                  — ALWAYS
      (a fresh LT equation always adds new information,
       so the full-decode cost is justified)
  (c) received_count >= last_attempted + post_k_retry_step

Non-repair symbols between backoff ticks now take the cheap path:
validate, buffer, return Ok(None). The schedule is K, K+1, K+2, K+4,
K+8, K+16, K+32 (cap) … so a slowly-arriving tail still completes in
bounded time.

## Regression tests (6)

  should_attempt_decode_first_crossover_fires
  should_attempt_decode_source_only_skips_between_backoff_ticks
  should_attempt_decode_repair_symbol_always_fires
  should_attempt_decode_fires_when_backoff_threshold_is_reached
  post_k_retry_step_doubles_on_insufficient_failure_and_caps
  round_trip_decode_after_backoff_coalesce_preserves_correctness
    (full encode -> decode round trip asserting the coalescer
     never blocks a decodable chain)

## Verified

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline \
    -p fcp-raptorq --lib
  -> 756 passed; 4 failed (was 750 + 6 new). The 4 pre-existing
     failures (decoder_*_duplicate / slot_cap / cancellation) are
     unrelated and reproduce on HEAD without these changes.

Closes flywheel_connectors-yu0l5.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
